task_completion.description: Create at least 4 new partition walls to separate the open plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan view in SweetHome3D for at least 4 newly drawn wall segments that divide the interior space.

task_completion.description: Create at least 5 distinct named rooms reflecting aquarium zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check the 2D plan for at least 5 defined room areas with visible names indicating aquarium zones (e.g., 'Ticketing', 'Main Exhibits', 'Touch Tank', 'Classroom', 'Gift Shop').

task_completion.description: Furnish the areas with shelving/display units, chairs, and desks/tables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine the 2D plan, 3D view, or the furniture list panel. Look for at least 10 shelving or display units (in Exhibits/Retail areas) AND at least 12 chairs and 4 desks or tables (in Education/Lobby areas).

task_completion.description: Draw a continuous visitor flow path with at least 4 distinct points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a polyline (a continuous drawn line) in the 2D plan view that traces a one-way walking route through the building, containing at least 4 distinct points or vertices.

task_completion.description: Include at least 3 text labels highlighting key features.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check the 2D plan view for at least 3 custom free-text labels placed to highlight specific features or areas of the aquarium design.

integrity.description: Agent used the SweetHome3D GUI to design the aquarium.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the SweetHome3D application windows, toolbars, and menus, rather than writing XML or running scripts in a terminal to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent worked on the correct starter file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SweetHome3D window title or recent files list should indicate that 'aquarium_shell_starter.sh3d' is the active project being modified.

integrity.description: Agent saved the project file normally.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the agent clicking 'File > Save' or using the save icon in the SweetHome3D toolbar at the end of the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying 'aquarium_shell_starter.sh3d' in SweetHome3D. The final design is evaluated on 5 criteria, each worth 20 points (100 points total): 1) Wall creation (>=4 new walls). 2) Room definition (>=5 named rooms). 3) Furniture placement (>=10 shelving/display units AND >=12 chairs + >=4 desks/tables). 4) Polyline drawing (a continuous visitor path with >=4 points). 5) Label placement (>=3 text labels). All elements must be visually verifiable in the SweetHome3D interface.
